title: Tailwind CSS Plugins Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the world of Tailwind CSS plugins and how they are used.

questions.question: Where do you add a Tailwind CSS plugin?
options: In your index.html file
In your tailwind.config.js plugins array
In your article.html script tag
In your CSS stylesheet.

questions.question: Which official Tailwind plugin is used to style raw HTML content like markdown?
options: @tailwindcss/forms
@tailwindcss/aspect-ratio
@tailwindcss/typography
@tailwindcss/line-clamp

questions.question: What's the main benefit of using @tailwindcss/forms?
options: It adds more animation to form elements.
It provides a robust CSS reset for standard HTML form elements.
It automatically validates form input.
It's the only way to use input fields in Tailwind.

questions.question: Can you only use official plugins provided by the Tailwind team?
options: Yes, for security reasons.
No, you can also use community-built plugins or even create your own.
Only if you're using Tailwind on a production site.
Yes, otherwise it's not considered standard.

questions.question: Is DaisyUI a Tailwind plugin?
options: Yes, it's a popular UI component library built as a Tailwind plugin.
No, it's a separate CSS framework altogether.
Only the version for React is a plugin.
It used to be, but now it's a standalone tool.
